rule.rationale: Async/await produces linear, readable code with proper stack traces and straightforward error handling via try/catch.

rule.desc: Use `Promise.all` for independent concurrent operations; use `Promise.allSettled` when partial failure is acceptable.
rule.rationale: Sequential awaits for independent work waste time. `Promise.all` fails fast; `Promise.allSettled` reports all results.

rule.desc: Accept `AbortSignal` for cancellable async operations. Propagate signals through the call chain.
rule.rationale: AbortSignal is the standard cancellation mechanism for fetch, streams, and long-running operations. Without it, abandoned work wastes resources.

rule.desc: Use typed event emitters or observable patterns instead of untyped string-based events.
rule.rationale: Typed events catch misspelled event names, wrong payload types, and missing handlers at compile time.

rule.desc: Use `for await...of` with async iterables for streaming data. Prefer async generators over manual iterator implementation.
rule.rationale: Async iterators handle backpressure naturally, compose well, and are the standard pattern for streaming data in Node.js and web streams.
